prompt: Which professors give the lowest average grades (strictest graders)?

inputData.question: Which professors give the lowest average grades (strictest graders)?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"Which professors give the lowest average grades (strictest graders)?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Which professors give the lowest average grades (strictest graders)?"},"startedAt":1771804233735,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"Compute each professor's average numeric grade by joining grades → enrollments → course_sections → professors, ignoring non-numeric grade_value entries, then list professors ordered by lowest average (strictest).","sql":"WITH numeric_grades AS (
  SELECT
    g.id AS grade_id,
    e.course_section_id AS course_section_id,
    CAST(g.grade_value::text AS numeric) AS numeric_grade
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
  WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_value::text ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'
)
SELECT
  p.id AS professor_id,
  (p.last_name || ', ' || p.first_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ng.numeric_grade)::numeric, 2) AS average_grade,
  COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) AS num_grades
FROM numeric_grades ng
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.id = ng.course_section_id
JOIN public.professors p ON p.id = cs.professor_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
HAVING COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) > 0
ORDER BY average_grade ASC, num_grades DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;","assumptions":["grade_value holds numeric grades as text/enum convertible to numeric (non-numeric entries are excluded)","Strictness = lower average numeric grade across all numeric-grade enrollments","Return all professors with at least one numeric grade, ordered by strictness (ties broken by more grades then name)"]},"endedAt":1771804310118},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"WITH numeric_grades AS (
  SELECT
    g.id AS grade_id,
    e.course_section_id AS course_section_id,
    CAST(g.grade_value::text AS numeric) AS numeric_grade
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
  WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_value::text ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'
)
SELECT
  p.id AS professor_id,
  (p.last_name || ', ' || p.first_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ng.numeric_grade)::numeric, 2) AS average_grade,
  COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) AS num_grades
FROM numeric_grades ng
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.id = ng.course_section_id
JOIN public.professors p ON p.id = cs.professor_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
HAVING COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) > 0
ORDER BY average_grade ASC, num_grades DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;"},"startedAt":1771804310123,"status":"success","output":{"ok":true,"sql":"WITH numeric_grades AS (

visualization.title: Professors with Lowest Average Grades (Strictest Graders)

resultRaw.sql:
WITH numeric_grades AS (
  SELECT
    g.id AS grade_id,
    e.course_section_id AS course_section_id,
    CAST(g.grade_value::text AS numeric) AS numeric_grade
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
  WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_value::text ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'
)
SELECT
  p.id AS professor_id,
  (p.last_name || ', ' || p.first_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ng.numeric_grade)::numeric, 2) AS average_grade,
  COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) AS num_grades
FROM numeric_grades ng
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.id = ng.course_section_id
JOIN public.professors p ON p.id = cs.professor_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
HAVING COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) > 0
ORDER BY average_grade ASC, num_grades DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;

visualization.title: Professors with Lowest Average Grades (Strictest Graders)

result.sql:
WITH numeric_grades AS (
  SELECT
    g.id AS grade_id,
    e.course_section_id AS course_section_id,
    CAST(g.grade_value::text AS numeric) AS numeric_grade
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
  WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_value::text ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'
)
SELECT
  p.id AS professor_id,
  (p.last_name || ', ' || p.first_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ng.numeric_grade)::numeric, 2) AS average_grade,
  COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) AS num_grades
FROM numeric_grades ng
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.id = ng.course_section_id
JOIN public.professors p ON p.id = cs.professor_id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
HAVING COUNT(ng.numeric_grade) > 0
ORDER BY average_grade ASC, num_grades DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;
